content_en:
“Then he may choose whichever issue he wishes.

As for the practice of some of the common people whenever they perform a voluntary prayer, or raising their hands so that with regard to some of them one would almost say that he has not supplicated. Because when the (obligatory) prayer begins and they are in the Tashahhud of the voluntary prayer, one observes them when they make the Taslim raising their hands and it is as if, and Allah knows better, they are simply raising them (without supplicating) and then they wipe their faces. All of this is to maintain a practice of supplication which they believe is legislated, but maintaining it to this degree is considered a form of innovation.

Q. 263. In some countries, after the obligatory prayers, they recite Surat Al-Fatihah and some statements of remembrance, and Ayat Al-Kursi in congregation; what is * the ruling in this practice?

"| Tahlil: Saying La Taha Illallah. '2] Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: What Supplication May be Chosen After the Tashahhud (800).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 496 PLY ots T Gsbs

A. The recitation of Surat Al-Fatihah, Ayat Al-Kursi and statements of remembrance after prayer in a loud voice in congregation is a form of innovation. Because what is well known from the Prophet #% and his Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, is that after the prayer, they would mention Allah in loud voices, but every one of them would mention Allah, the Most High alone, without joining together. Raising the voice in remembrance after the obligatory prayers is a Sunnah, as confirmed in Sahih Al-Bukhari, on the authority of Ibn ‘Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them, who said: “At the time of the Prophet ##, the people used to raise their voices when they had completed the obligatory prayers.”!")

As for the recitation of Surat Al-Fatihah after the prayers whether it be quietly or out loud I know of no Hadith from the Prophet #% in this regard; it has only been reported in the Hadith that one should recite Ayat Al-Kursi, Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad (i.e. Surat Al-Ikhlas) and Al-Mu‘awithatan (i.e. Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas).

Q. 264. If a person feared that if he answered the call of nature, he would miss the congregational prayer, should he pray while he is holding it in, in order to catch the congregational prayer, or should he answer the cal] of nature, even if he misses the congregational prayer?

A. He should answer the call of nature and then make ablution, even if he missed the congregational prayer, because this is an excuse and the Prophet 3 said:

") Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim: It was reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: The Remembrance After the Prayer (841); and by Muslim in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Remembrance After the Prayer (583).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 497 BMI! soli

“There is no prayer when food is served, nor when he is suppressing the two filths.’""}

Q. 265. What is the ruling on closing the eyes in prayer?

A. Closing the eyes in prayer is disliked, because it contradicts the practice of the Prophet #%, unless there is a reason for it, such as if there were geometric patterns on the wall in front of him, or on the carpet, or if there was a strong light in front of him which hurt his eyes. The important thing is that if the closing of the eyes was for a reason, then there is no objection to it, but otherwise, it is disliked. And whoever wished to study more about it should refer to the book Zadul-Ma‘ad by Ibn Qayyim may Allah, the Most High have mercy on him.

Q. 266. Does cracking the knuckles in prayer forgetfully invalidate the prayer?

A. Cracking the knuckles does not invalidate the prayer, but it is a vain deed, and if it is in the congregational prayers, it must cause annoyance to those who hear it, and so the harm of it is greater than if there were no one around him.

And I would like to take this opportunity to say that movements in prayer fall into five categories:

(i) Obligatory Movements, (ii) movements which are a Sunnah, (iii) movements which are disliked, (iv) forbidden movements, and (v) permissible movements.

As for the obligatory movements, they are based on obligatory actions in the prayer, such as when a person stands in prayer, then he remembers that there is something impure on his _ headdress; in that case, it is incumbent upon him to remove this headdress, so this movement is obligatory. The evidence for that is when Jibril, peace be upon him, came to the Prophet

") Reported by Muslim in the Book of the Masjids, in the Chapter: The Dislike of Praying When Food is Served 1:393, Hadith 67 (560).

kar slam 498 Pe OST syle

and informed him that there was some filth on his shoes and he # removed them during the prayer and continued with his prayer."'! So, this movement is obligatory, and the rule governing it is that doing it involves an obligatory action in * the prayer or the abandonment of something forbidden.

As for the movements which are a Sunnah, they are those upon whose performance the perfection of the prayer depends. Such as moving up in the row when a space opens up. A person moves closer to his neighbor in order to close this space; this is a Sunnah and so this action is sanctioned by the Islamic Law.

As for disliked movement, it is unnecessary movement which has no relation to the perfection of the prayer.

As for forbidden movement, it is frequent and repeated movement, such as when a person does vain things while he is standing, while he is bowing, while he is prostrating and while he is sitting, so that the prayer deviates from its prescribed form. This movement is forbidden, because it invalidates the prayer.

As for permissible movements, they are anything not included in the above, such as when a person feels an itch and so he scratches it, or his headdress falls over his eye, so he raises it; these are among the permissible actions, or someone asks his permission and so he raises his hand in order to give his permission. These are among the permitted movements.

Q. 267. What is the ruling on the Sutrah? And what is the limit of it?

A. The Sutrah in prayer is an emphisized Sunnah, except for the one who is led in prayer, because it is not legislated for the one

'] Reported by Abu Dawud in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: Praying in Shoes, Hadith (650); and declared authentic by Ibn Khuzaimah 1:384 (786); and it was reported by Ibn Hibban 5:560 (2185).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 499 Beall sshd

being led in prayer to take a Sutrah; the Sutrah of the Imam is sufficient for him.

As for the limit of it, the Prophet #% was asked about it and he said:

(Pole Bo “Such as the back of the saddle of his riding mount.”""!

But this is the highest that it may be, and anything less than it will suffice, for it has been reported in a Hadith: “If one of you is in prayer, let him put a Sutrah in front of him, even if it is an arrow'?!

It has been reported in another Hadith reported by Abu Dawud with a Hasan chain of narrators:

“Whoever does not find anything, he should draw a line (in the dust).”"?)

Hafiz Ibn Hajar said in Bulugh Al-Maram: Those who claim that it is Mudtarib'*! are not correct, for there is no weakness in the

‘4 Reported by Muslim in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: The Sutrah of the Worshipper 1:358, Hadith 241 (499).

(21 Reported by Ibn Khuzaimah in the Chapters on the Sutrah of the Worshipper 2:12 (811) and it was reported by Ahmad 3:404 (and Ar-

') Reported by Ibn Khuzaimah in the aforementioned location, Hadith (811); and it was reported by Abu Dawud in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: What the Worshipper May Use as a Sutrah; and it was reported by Ibn Majah in the Book of the Jgamah of the Prayer, in the Chapter: The Sutrah of the Worshipper (943); and refer to Sahih Ibn

'4) Mudtarib: A Hadith which is narrated in a number of contradictory forms, in such a manner that it is impossible to reconcile them and the

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 500 PLY OISyt Gold

Hadith which would necessitate its rejection, so we say that the minimum for it is a line and the maximum is the back of a riding beast’s saddle.

Q. 268. What is the ruling on passing directly in front of a worshipper in prayer in the Sacred Masjid (in Makkah), whether the worshipper is performing an obligatory prayer or a supererogatory one, and whether he is being led in prayer or praying alone?

A. As for passing in front of a person who is being led in prayer, there is no objection to it in the Sacred Masjid, nor in any other Masjid, because Ibn ‘Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him, came to the Prophet #% when he was in Mina and he was leading the people in prayer without a wall in front of him, and he passed in front of the row while he was riding on his donkey, and no one censured him.!?!

But if the worshipper was the Imam or a person praying alone, then it is not permissible for anyone to pass directly in front of him, neither in the sacred Masjid nor in any other Masjid, due to the general nature of the evidences, and there is no evidence to single out Makkah or the Sacred Masjid and to prove that passing directly in front of a worshipper in prayer there does not harm, and that the one who does so is not guilty of sin.

Q. 269. What is the ruling on placing an electric heater in front of the worshippers during the performance of their prayers? And has any legal prohibition been reported

chains of narrators of all of them are of equal strength, so that it is not possible to say which of them is more correct.

") Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Knowledge, in Chapter 19: When it is Correct to Accept a Child’s Report (76); and in other locations, and it was reported by Muslim in the Book of Prayer in Chapter 47: The Sutrah of the Worshipper in Prayer 1:361, Hadith 354

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 501 Ball old
content_id:
"Kemudian ia boleh memilih masalah mana saja yang ia kehendaki.

Adapun kebiasaan sebagian orang awam setiap kali mereka melaksanakan salah sunnah, atau mengangkat tangan mereka sehingga terhadap sebagian dari mereka hampir dapat dikatakan bahwa ia tidak berdu'a. Karena ketika salah (wajib) dimulai dan mereka sedang dalam Tashahhud salah sunnah, seseorang melihat mereka ketika mengucapkan salam mengangkat tangan mereka dan seolah-olah, wallahu a'lam, mereka hanya mengangkatnya (tanpa berdu'a) kemudian mengusap wajah mereka. Semua ini adalah untuk mempertahankan kebiasaan berdu'a yang mereka yakini disyariatkan, tetapi mempertahankannya sampai tingkat seperti ini dianggap sebagai bentuk bid'ah.

Q. 263. Di beberapa negara, setelah salah wajib, mereka membaca Surat Al-Fatihah dan beberapa kalimat dhikr, serta Ayat Al-Kursi secara berjamaah; apakah hukum amalan ini?

"| Tahlil: Mengucapkan La Ilaha Illallah. '2] Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, dalam Bab: Du'a Apa yang Boleh Dipilih Setelah Tashahhud (800).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 496 PLY ots T Gsbs

A. Membaca Surat Al-Fatihah, Ayat Al-Kursi dan kalimat-kalimat dhikr setelah salah dengan suara keras secara berjamaah adalah bentuk bid'ah. Karena yang diketahui dari Nabi ﷺ dan para Sahabat beliau, semoga Allah meridhai mereka, adalah bahwa setelah salah, mereka berdhikr kepada Allah dengan suara keras, tetapi setiap orang dari mereka berdhikr kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi secara sendiri-sendiri, tanpa bersama-sama. Mengeraskan suara dalam dhikr setelah salah wajib adalah Sunnah, sebagaimana dikukuhkan dalam Sahih Al-Bukhari, dari Ibnu 'Abbas, semoga Allah meridhai keduanya, yang berkata: "Pada masa Nabi ﷺ, orang-orang biasa mengeraskan suara mereka ketika mereka telah menyelesaikan salah wajib."

Adapun membaca Surat Al-Fatihah setelah salah, baik secara pelan maupun keras, saya tidak mengetahui adanya hadith dari Nabi ﷺ dalam hal ini; yang diriwayatkan dalam hadith hanyalah bahwa seseorang hendaknya membaca Ayat Al-Kursi, Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad (yaitu Surat Al-Ikhlas) dan Al-Mu'awwidzatan (yaitu Surat Al-Falaq dan Surat An-Nas).

Q. 264. Jika seseorang khawatir bahwa jika ia memenuhi panggilan alam, ia akan ketinggalan salah berjamaah, apakah ia harus salah sambil menahannya demi mendapatkan salah berjamaah, atau haruskah ia memenuhi panggilan alam meskipun ia ketinggalan salah berjamaah?

A. Ia harus memenuhi panggilan alam terlebih dahulu kemudian berwudhu, meskipun ia ketinggalan salah berjamaah, karena ini adalah uzur dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim: Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, dalam Bab: Dhikr Setelah Salah (841); dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid, dalam Bab: Dhikr Setelah Salah (583).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 497 BMI! soli

"Tidak ada salah ketika makanan dihidangkan, dan tidak pula ketika seseorang menahan dua kotoran (buang air besar dan kecil)."

Q. 265. Apa hukum memejamkan mata dalam salah?

A. Memejamkan mata dalam salah adalah makruh, karena bertentangan dengan amalan Nabi ﷺ, kecuali jika ada alasan untuk itu, seperti jika ada pola-pola geometris di dinding di depannya, atau di karpet, atau jika ada cahaya kuat di depannya yang menyakiti matanya. Yang penting adalah jika memejamkan mata itu karena suatu alasan, maka tidak ada keberatan terhadapnya, tetapi jika tidak, maka itu makruh. Dan barangsiapa ingin mempelajarinya lebih lanjut, hendaklah merujuk kepada kitab Zadul-Ma'ad karya Ibnu Qayyim semoga Allah Yang Maha Tinggi merahmatinya.

Q. 266. Apakah membunyikan jari-jari (ruas jari) dalam salah karena lupa membatalkan salah?

A. Membunyikan ruas jari tidak membatalkan salah, tetapi itu adalah perbuatan sia-sia, dan jika dilakukan dalam salah berjamaah, pasti akan mengganggu orang-orang yang mendengarnya, sehingga mudharatnya lebih besar dibandingkan jika tidak ada orang di sekitarnya.

Dan saya ingin mengambil kesempatan ini untuk mengatakan bahwa gerakan dalam salah terbagi menjadi lima kategori:

(i) Gerakan Wajib, (ii) gerakan yang merupakan Sunnah, (iii) gerakan yang makruh, (iv) gerakan yang haram, dan (v) gerakan yang mubah.

Adapun gerakan wajib, ia didasarkan pada perbuatan wajib dalam salah, seperti ketika seseorang berdiri dalam salah, kemudian ia teringat bahwa ada najis pada penutup kepalanya; dalam hal ini, ia wajib melepas penutup kepala tersebut, maka gerakan ini adalah wajib. Dalilnya adalah ketika Jibril, 'alaihissalam, datang kepada Nabi ﷺ

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid, dalam Bab: Makruhnya Salah Ketika Makanan Dihidangkan 1:393, Hadith 67 (560).

kar slam 498 Pe OST syle

dan memberitahukan bahwa ada najis pada sandalnya dan beliau ﷺ melepasnya saat salah dan meneruskan salatnya. Maka gerakan ini adalah wajib, dan kaidah yang mengaturnya adalah bahwa melakukannya melibatkan perbuatan wajib dalam salah atau meninggalkan sesuatu yang haram.

Adapun gerakan yang merupakan Sunnah, ia adalah gerakan yang kesempurnaan salah bergantung pada pelaksanaannya. Seperti bergerak maju dalam shaf ketika ada celah yang terbuka. Seseorang bergerak mendekati tetangganya untuk menutup celah tersebut; ini adalah Sunnah dan oleh karena itu tindakan ini disahkan oleh Syariat Islam.

Adapun gerakan makruh, ia adalah gerakan yang tidak perlu dan tidak ada hubungannya dengan kesempurnaan salah.

Adapun gerakan haram, ia adalah gerakan yang sering dan berulang-ulang, seperti ketika seseorang melakukan hal-hal sia-sia saat berdiri, saat ruku', saat sujud dan saat duduk, sehingga salah menyimpang dari bentuk yang disyariatkan. Gerakan ini haram karena membatalkan salah.

Adapun gerakan mubah, ia adalah segala sesuatu yang tidak termasuk dalam kategori di atas, seperti ketika seseorang merasakan gatal lalu menggaruknya, atau penutup kepalanya jatuh menutupi matanya lalu ia mengangkatnya; ini termasuk perbuatan yang mubah, atau seseorang meminta izinnya lalu ia mengangkat tangannya untuk memberikan izin. Ini termasuk gerakan yang diperbolehkan.

Q. 267. Apa hukum sutrah? Dan apa batasannya?

A. Sutrah dalam salah adalah Sunnah yang sangat ditekankan, kecuali bagi orang yang menjadi makmum, karena tidak disyariatkan bagi makmum

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dalam Kitab Salah, dalam Bab: Salah dengan Memakai Sandal, Hadith (650); dan dinyatakan sahih oleh Ibnu Khuzaimah 1:384 (786); dan diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Hibban 5:560 (2185).

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 499 Beall sshd

untuk mengambil sutrah; sutrah imam sudah cukup baginya.

Adapun batasannya, Nabi ﷺ ditanya tentangnya dan beliau bersabda:

"Seperti bagian belakang pelana tunggangannya."

Tetapi ini adalah batas tertingginya, dan apa pun yang kurang dari itu sudah cukup, karena telah diriwayatkan dalam sebuah hadith: "Jika salah seorang dari kalian sedang salah, hendaklah ia menaruh sutrah di depannya, meskipun hanya sebuah anak panah."

Telah diriwayatkan dalam hadith lain yang diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dengan sanad hasan:

"Barangsiapa tidak menemukan sesuatu, hendaklah ia menggaris sebuah garis (di tanah)."

Al-Hafizh Ibnu Hajar berkata dalam Bulugh Al-Maram: Mereka yang mengklaim bahwa hadith itu mudhtarib tidak benar, karena tidak ada kelemahan dalam

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salah, dalam Bab: Sutrah Orang yang Salah 1:358, Hadith 241 (499).

Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Khuzaimah dalam Bab-bab tentang Sutrah Orang yang Salah 2:12 (811) dan diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad 3:404 (dan Ar-

Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Khuzaimah di tempat yang disebutkan sebelumnya, Hadith (811); dan diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dalam Kitab Salah, dalam Bab: Apa yang Boleh Digunakan Orang yang Salah sebagai Sutrah; dan diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Majah dalam Kitab Iqamah Salah, dalam Bab: Sutrah Orang yang Salah (943); dan lihat Sahih Ibnu

Mudhtarib: Hadith yang diriwayatkan dalam beberapa bentuk yang saling bertentangan, sedemikian rupa sehingga tidak mungkin untuk mendamaikannya dan

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 500 PLY OISyt Gold

hadith yang mengharuskan penolakannya, maka kami katakan bahwa batas minimumnya adalah sebuah garis dan batas maksimumnya adalah bagian belakang pelana tunggangan.

Q. 268. Apa hukum lewat langsung di depan orang yang sedang salah di Masjidil Haram (di Makkah), baik orang yang salah itu melaksanakan salah wajib maupun salah sunnah, dan baik ia menjadi makmum maupun salah sendirian?

A. Adapun lewat di depan orang yang menjadi makmum dalam salat, maka tidak ada keberatan mengenai hal itu di Masjidil Haram maupun di masjid lainnya, karena Ibn 'Abbas, semoga Allah meridhainya, datang kepada Nabi ﷺ ketika beliau berada di Mina dan beliau sedang mengimami orang-orang dalam salat tanpa sutrah di depannya, dan ia lewat di depan shaf sambil mengendarai keledainya, dan tidak ada seorang pun yang menegurnya.

Namun jika yang salat itu adalah imam atau orang yang salat sendirian, maka tidak diperbolehkan bagi siapa pun untuk lewat tepat di depannya, baik di Masjidil Haram maupun di masjid lainnya, karena sifat umum dari dalil-dalil yang ada, dan tidak ada dalil yang mengkhususkan Makkah atau Masjidil Haram serta membuktikan bahwa lewat tepat di depan orang yang sedang salat di sana tidak membahayakan, dan bahwa orang yang melakukannya tidak berdosa.

Q. 269. Apa hukum meletakkan pemanas listrik di depan orang-orang yang salat selama pelaksanaan salat mereka? Dan apakah ada larangan syar'i yang diriwayatkan

sanad-sanad dari semuanya memiliki kekuatan yang sama, sehingga tidak memungkinkan untuk mengatakan mana di antara mereka yang lebih sahih.

") Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Ilmu, pada Bab 19: Ketika Sah Menerima Kabar dari Seorang Anak (76); dan di tempat-tempat lain, dan diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat pada Bab 47: Sutrah bagi Orang yang Salat 1:361, Hadith 354

content_ar: “Then he may choose whichever issue he wishes. As for the practice of some of the common people whenever they perform a voluntary الصلاة, or raising their hands so that with regard to some of them one would almost say that he has not supplicated. Because when the (واجب) الصلاة begins and they are in the Tashahhud of the voluntary الصلاة, one observes them when they make the Taslim raising their hands and it is as if, and الله knows better, they are simply raising them (without supplicating) and then they wipe their faces. All of this is to maintain a practice of الدعاء which they believe is legislated, but maintaining it to this degree is considered a form of innovation. Q. 263. In some countries, after the واجب prayers, they recite Surat Al-Fatihah and some statements of remembrance, and Ayat Al-Kursi in congregation; what is * the ruling in this practice? "| Tahlil: Saying La Taha Illallah. '2] Reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: What الدعاء May be Chosen After the Tashahhud (800). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 496 PLY ots T Gsbs A. The recitation of Surat Al-Fatihah, Ayat Al-Kursi and statements of remembrance after الصلاة in a loud voice in congregation is a form of innovation. Because what is well known from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% and his الصحابة, رضي الله عنهم, is that after the الصلاة, they would mention الله in loud voices, but every one of them would mention الله, the Most High alone, without joining together. Raising the voice in remembrance after the واجب prayers is a السنة, as confirmed in Sahih البخاري, on the authority of Ibn ‘Abbas, رضي الله عنهم, who said: “At the time of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ##, the people used to raise their voices when they had completed the واجب prayers.”!") As for the recitation of Surat Al-Fatihah after the prayers whether it be quietly or out loud I know of no الحديث from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% in this regard; it has only been reported in الحديث النبوي الشريف that one should recite Ayat Al-Kursi, Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad (i.e. Surat Al-الإخلاص) and Al-Mu‘awithatan (i.e. Surat Al-Falaq and Surat An-Nas). Q. 264. If a person feared that if he answered the call of nature, he would miss the congregational الصلاة, should he pray while he is holding it in, in order to catch the congregational الصلاة, or should he answer the cal] of nature, even if he misses the congregational الصلاة? A. He should answer the call of nature and then make ablution, even if he missed the congregational الصلاة, because this is an excuse and النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 3 said: ") Reported by البخاري and المسلم: It was reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: The Remembrance After the الصلاة (841); and by المسلم in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Remembrance After the الصلاة (583). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 497 BMI! soli “There is no الصلاة when food is served, nor when he is suppressing the two filths.’""} Q. 265. What is the ruling on closing the eyes in الصلاة? A. Closing the eyes in الصلاة is مكروه, because it contradicts the practice of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #%, unless there is a reason for it, such as if there were geometric patterns on the wall in front of him, or on the carpet, or if there was a strong light in front of him which hurt his eyes. The important thing is that if the closing of the eyes was for a reason, then there is no objection to it, but otherwise, it is مكروه. And whoever wished to study more about it should refer to the book Zadul-Ma‘ad by Ibn Qayyim may الله, the Most High have mercy on him. Q. 266. Does cracking the knuckles in الصلاة forgetfully invalidate the الصلاة? A. Cracking the knuckles does not invalidate the الصلاة, but it is a vain deed, and if it is in the congregational prayers, it must cause annoyance to those who hear it, and so the harm of it is greater than if there were no one around him. And I would like to take this opportunity to say that movements in الصلاة fall into five categories: (i) واجب Movements, (ii) movements which are a السنة, (iii) movements which are مكروه, (iv) محرم movements, and (v) مباح movements. As for the واجب movements, they are based on واجب actions in the الصلاة, such as when a person stands in الصلاة, then he remembers that there is something impure on his _ headdress; in that case, it is incumbent upon him to remove this headdress, so this movement is واجب. The evidence for that is when Jibril, peace be upon him, came to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ") Reported by المسلم in the Book of the Masjids, in the Chapter: The Dislike of Praying When Food is Served 1:393, الحديث 67 (560). kar slam 498 Pe OST syle and informed him that there was some filth on his shoes and he # removed them during the الصلاة and continued with his الصلاة."'! So, this movement is واجب, and the rule governing it is that doing it involves an واجب action in * the الصلاة or the abandonment of something محرم. As for the movements which are a السنة, they are those upon whose performance the perfection of the الصلاة depends. Such as moving up in the row when a space opens up. A person moves closer to his neighbor in order to close this space; this is a السنة and so this action is sanctioned by the الإسلامي Law. As for مكروه movement, it is unnecessary movement which has no relation to the perfection of the الصلاة. As for محرم movement, it is frequent and repeated movement, such as when a person does vain things while he is standing, while he is bowing, while he is prostrating and while he is sitting, so that the الصلاة deviates from its prescribed form. This movement is محرم, because it invalidates the الصلاة. As for مباح movements, they are anything not included in the above, such as when a person feels an itch and so he scratches it, or his headdress falls over his eye, so he raises it; these are among the مباح actions, or someone asks his permission and so he raises his hand in order to give his permission. These are among the permitted movements. Q. 267. What is the ruling on the Sutrah? And what is the limit of it? A. The Sutrah in الصلاة is an emphisized السنة, except for the one who is led in الصلاة, because it is not legislated for the one '] Reported by أبو داود in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: Praying in Shoes, الحديث (650); and declared صحيح by Ibn Khuzaimah 1:384 (786); and it was reported by Ibn Hibban 5:560 (2185). الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 499 Beall sshd being led in الصلاة to take a Sutrah; the Sutrah of the الإمام is sufficient for him. As for the limit of it, النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% was asked about it and he said: (Pole Bo “Such as the back of the saddle of his riding mount.”""! But this is the highest that it may be, and anything less than it will suffice, for it has been reported in a الحديث: “If one of you is in الصلاة, let him put a Sutrah in front of him, even if it is an arrow'?! It has been reported in another الحديث reported by أبو داود with a Hasan chain of narrators: “Whoever does not find anything, he should draw a line (in the dust).”"?) (21 Reported by Ibn Khuzaimah in the Chapters on the Sutrah of the Worshipper 2:12 (811) and it was reported by Ahmad 3:404 (and Ar- ') Reported by Ibn Khuzaimah in the aforementioned location, الحديث (811); and it was reported by أبو داود in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: What the Worshipper May Use as a Sutrah; and it was reported by ابن ماجه in the Book of the Jgamah of the الصلاة, in the Chapter: The Sutrah of the Worshipper (943); and refer to Sahih Ibn '4) Mudtarib: A الحديث which is روى in a number of contradictory forms, in such a manner that it is impossible to reconcile them and the الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 500 PLY OISyt Gold الحديث which would necessitate its rejection, so we say that the minimum for it is a line and the maximum is the back of a riding beast’s saddle. Q. 268. What is the ruling on passing directly in front of a worshipper in الصلاة in the Sacred المسجد (in مكة المكرمة), whether the worshipper is performing an واجب الصلاة or a supererogatory one, and whether he is being led in الصلاة or praying alone? A. As for passing in front of a person who is being led in الصلاة, there is no objection to it in the Sacred المسجد, nor in any other المسجد, because Ibn ‘Abbas, رضي الله عنه, came to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% when he was in Mina and he was leading the people in الصلاة without a wall in front of him, and he passed in front of the row while he was riding on his donkey, and no one censured him.!?! But if the worshipper was the الإمام or a person praying alone, then it is not مباح for anyone to pass directly in front of him, neither in the sacred المسجد nor in any other المسجد, due to the general nature of the evidences, and there is no evidence to single out مكة المكرمة or the Sacred المسجد and to prove that passing directly in front of a worshipper in الصلاة there does not harm, and that the one who does so is not guilty of sin. Q. 269. What is the ruling on placing an electric heater in front of the worshippers during the performance of their prayers? And has any legal prohibition been reported chains of narrators of all of them are of equal strength, so that it is not possible to say which of them is more correct.
